required_groups.description: Alice found a lovely/beautiful garden with bright flowers and cool fountains.
Alice met a Caterpillar sitting on a mushroom and smoking a hookah.
The Caterpillar kept asking Alice 'Who are you?'
Eating or drinking caused Alice to change in size (shrinking or growing).
The Queen enforced chaotic croquet rules (e.g., executing players, no turns, playing all at once).
Live flamingos were used as mallets.
Live hedgehogs were used as balls.
Soldiers doubled up to serve as arches.
The Mad Hatter, March Hare, and Dormouse sat at the tea party.
Time was stuck because the Hatter quarreled with Time.
Time remains stuck at six o'clock (always tea-time).
A paper or letter with verses (nonsense poem) was presented as evidence.
The writing was not in the Knave's own handwriting.
The verses were unsigned / had no signature at the end.
The Queen of Hearts threatened to execute characters by saying 'Off with his/her head!'
The Queen targeted the gardeners (Five, Seven, and Two).
The Queen targeted the Mad Hatter.
The poem 'How doth the little crocodile' recited by Alice
The Mouse's long and sad tale
The poem 'You Are Old, Father William' recited by Alice
The lullaby 'Speak roughly to your little boy' sung by the Duchess
The song/poem 'Twinkle, twinkle, little bat' sung by the Hatter
The Lobster Quadrille song performed by the Mock Turtle
The altered poem 'Tis the Voice of the Sluggard' recited by Alice
The song 'Beautiful Soup' sung by the Mock Turtle
The tart accusation rhyme/poem read by the White Rabbit
The trial verse/poem 'They told me you had been to her' read by the White Rabbit
Initially Alice experienced uncontrolled size changes and confusion/crying.
She learned to control her size by eating pieces of the mushroom.
During the trial, Alice grew to her full size and gained confidence, defying the Queen/court.
Mock Turtle's school subjects include Reeling, Writhing, and Arithmetic (Ambition, Distraction, Uglification, Derision).
The Drawling-master was an old conger-eel.
Subjects taught by the eel were Drawling, Stretching, and Fainting in Coils.
